searchDocs.title: lifetime
searchDocs.content: lifetime A utility for managing the lifetime of objects. When the end of the lifetime is reached, element:Destroy() is called. local lifetime = broid.lifetime local new = broid.new local element = new("Circle", { [lifetime] = 5, -- Lifetime of 5 seconds })

searchDocs.title: value​
searchDocs.content: </> value.value: any The value's value.

searchDocs.title: rendered
searchDocs.content: rendered A utility for creating rendered states that always update every frame. local rendered = broid.rendered local new = broid.new local element = new("Circle", { x = rendered(function(deltaTime) -- This function is called every frame. deltaTime is the time since the last frame. return os.clock() % 100 end), })

searchDocs.title: computed
searchDocs.content: computed A state that reacts to other states. local computed = broid.computed local value = broid.value local foo = value.new(1) local bar = value.new(2) local sum = computed(function(use) return use(foo) + use(bar) end) print(sum.value) -- 3 foo.value = 3 print(sum.value) -- 5

searchDocs.title: drawMode​
searchDocs.content: </> enum.drawMode: : { fill: "fill", line: "line" } The draw mode for shapes. "fill" will fill the shape, while "line" will only draw the outline.

searchDocs.title: alignMode​
searchDocs.content: </> enum.alignMode: : { left: "left", center: "center", right: "right", justify: "justify" } The alignment mode for text. "left" will align text to the left, "center" will center the text, "right" will align text to the right, and "justify" will justify the text.

searchDocs.title: destroyed
searchDocs.content: destroyed A declaration in new that calls a function when an element is destroyed. local destroyed = broid.destroyed local new = broid.new local element = new("Circle", { [destroyed] = function() print("Element was destroyed!") end }) element:Destroy() -- Prints "Element was destroyed!"

searchDocs.title: getValue
searchDocs.content: getValue A utility for retrieving values from different types of objects. local value = broid.value local getValue = broid.getValue local foo = value.new(1) print(getValue(foo)) -- 1

searchDocs.title: isState
searchDocs.content: isState A utility for checking if a value is a state object. local value = broid.value local isState = broid.isState local foo = value.new(1) print(isState(foo)) -- true print(isState(123)) -- false print(isState("Hello")) -- false

searchDocs.title: spring
searchDocs.content: spring A spring state for animating objects. local spring = broid.spring local new = broid.new local value = broid.value local target = value(0) new("circle", { x = spring(target, 30, 1) -- Animates target with a speed of 30 and dampening of 1 }) target.value = 100 -- Animate to x = 100

searchDocs.title: tags
searchDocs.content: tags A declaration used in new for declaring tags on an element, which can be retrieved with element:GetTags() local new = broid.new local tags = broid.tags local circle = new("circle", { [tags] = {"x", "y", "z"} }) print(circle:GetTags()[1]) -- "x"

searchDocs.title: color3​
searchDocs.content: </> type color3 = {[number]: number} The color constructed type.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> color3.new( r: : number,-- Red value between 0 and 1 g: : number,-- Green value between 0 and 1 b: : number-- Blue value between 0 and 1 ) → color3

searchDocs.title: fromRGB​
searchDocs.content: </> color3.fromRGB( r: : number,-- Red value between 0 and 255 g: : number,-- Green value between 0 and 255 b: : number-- Blue value between 0 and 255 ) → color3

searchDocs.title: x​
searchDocs.content: </> scale.x(xScale: : number) → value A function that takes a scale factor for the x-coordinate and returns a value that updates based on the window's width. local scale = broid.scale local xScale = scale.x(0.5) -- Scale the x-coordinate by 50% print(xScale.value) -- Assuming a window width of 800, this will print 400.

searchDocs.title: y​
searchDocs.content: </> scale.y(yScale: : number) → value A function that takes a scale factor for the y-coordinate and returns a value that updates based on the window's height. local scale = broid.scale local yScale = scale.y(0.8) -- Scale the y-coordinate by 80% print(yScale.value) -- Assuming a window height of 600, this will print 480.

searchDocs.title: event​
searchDocs.content: </> type event = symbol An event type that can be used as a declaration in elements. When the event is fired, the function connected to it will be called.

searchDocs.title: mouseButton1Down​
searchDocs.content: </> event.mouseButton1Down: : event Fired when the left mouse button is pressed down on an element.

searchDocs.title: mouseButton1Up​
searchDocs.content: </> event.mouseButton1Up: : event Fired when the left mouse button is released on an element.

searchDocs.title: mouseButton2Down​
searchDocs.content: </> event.mouseButton2Down: : event Fired when the right mouse button is pressed down on an element.

searchDocs.title: mouseButton2Up​
searchDocs.content: </> event.mouseButton2Up: : event Fired when the right mouse button is released on an element.

searchDocs.title: mouseButton3Down​
searchDocs.content: </> event.mouseButton3Down: : event Fired when the middle mouse button is pressed down on an element.

searchDocs.title: mouseButton3Up​
searchDocs.content: </> event.mouseButton3Up: : event Fired when the middle mouse button is released on an element.

searchDocs.title: mouseEnter​
searchDocs.content: </> event.mouseEnter: : event Fired when the mouse enters an element.

searchDocs.title: mouseLeave​
searchDocs.content: </> event.mouseLeave: : event Fired when the mouse leaves an element.

searchDocs.title: InnerScope​
searchDocs.content: </> scope:InnerScope(newScopedObjects: : {[any]: any}?) → scope Creates a scope that extends from the current scope. When the original scope is cleaned up, the inner scope will also be cleaned up. local innerScope = myScope:InnerScope({myCustomObject = myCustomObject})

searchDocs.title: AddObject​
searchDocs.content: </> scope:AddObject(object: : any) → () Adds an object to the scope. When the scope is destroyed, the object will also be destroyed. myScope:AddObject(myCustomObject)

searchDocs.title: RemoveObject​
searchDocs.content: </> scope:RemoveObject(object: : any) → () Removes an object from the scope. The object will be destroyed and will no longer be part of the scope. myScope:RemoveObject(myCustomObject)

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> scope:Destroy() → () Destroys the scope and all objects within it. myScope:Destroy()

searchDocs.title: circle​
searchDocs.content: </> type circle = { drawMode: enum.drawMode, x: number, y: number, radius: number, segments: number, anchorPointX: number, anchorPointY: number, visible: boolean } A basic circle element based on love.graphics.circle.

searchDocs.title: drawable​
searchDocs.content: </> type drawable = { drawable: Drawable, x: number, y: number, rotation: numbeR, scaleX: number, scaleY: number, originOffsetX: number, originOffsetY: number, shearX: number, shearY: number, anchorPointX: number, anchorPointY: number, visible: boolean }

searchDocs.title: text​
searchDocs.content: </> type text = { text: string | table, x: number, y: number, limit: number, align: alignMode, rotation: number, scaleX: number, scaleY: number, originX: number, originY: number, shearX: number, shearY: number, anchorPointX: number, anchorPointY: number, font: Font } Basic text based on love.graphics.printf.

searchDocs.title: WIP
searchDocs.content: WIP These docs serve as a WIP while I develop this. Keep tabs in the Love2D Discord server for when I update this!

searchDocs.title: You're saving time with less work​
searchDocs.content: It's almost a miracle. Gone are the days of manually doing everything Broid can do... because Broid can do it!

searchDocs.title: Supported elements​
searchDocs.content: Refer to the below table to know what is supported, and to what degree  Element type	Supports render	Supports inputArc	✖	✖ Circle	✔	✔ Drawable	✔	✔ Mesh	✖	✖ Array Texture	✖	✖ Quad	✖	✖ Ellipse	✖	✖ Line	✖	✖ Points	✖	✖ Polygon	✖	✖ Text	✔	✔ Rectangle	✖	✖ Stencil	✖	✖ Triangle	✖	✖
